definition:
1. Dep.: 
2. to set up as a pretext  or excuse, allege by way of excuse, plead in excuse , with accusative, (Theognis Elegiacus), etc.; with infinitive to allege as an excuse that . . , (Demosthenes Orator):—;absolute to make excuses , (Thucydides):—;aor1 προφασισθῆναι in passive sense, to be used as a pretext , (Thucydides) 
3. to allege  (by way of accusation) that , (Plato Philosophus) 
from πρόφᾶσις (ML)
